steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: In right triangle XYZ with the right angle at Z, the two acute angles X and Y satisfy $X + Y = 90^\circ$, making them complementary.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: By the co-function identity, $\sin X = \cos(90^\circ - X) = \cos Y$. So $\sin X$ equals $\cos Y$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Verify directly using sides: label the sides as $XZ = a$, $YZ = b$, hypotenuse $XY = c$. Then $\sin X = \frac{b}{c}$ (opposite over hypotenuse). For angle Y, adjacent = $YZ = b$ and hypotenuse = $c$, so $\cos Y = \frac{b}{c}$. Confirmed equal.

steps.title: Step 4
